🔥Trademark law does not let a trademark owner exert its trademark rights to stop news reporting about it or its products or services. You see proof of this everyday on the front pages of newspapers, the homepages of news websites, and countless blogs. Mainstream reporters and non-traditional journalists routinely report on earnings announcements, job lay-offs, and accounting scandals without worrying that they are infringing or diluting the trademarks of the companies and organizations they report on. There are several legal bases for this result: there is no risk of confusion between the news source and the trademark owner; nominative fair use protects this use of the trademark owner's mark; and the federal dilution statute expressly exempts "news reporting and news commentary" from a dilution claim. See 15 U.S.C. § 1125(c)(3)(B). As noted above, one court has held that a blogger's critical commentary on a company qualified as "news reporting and news commentary." See BidZirk, LLC v. Smith, 2007 WL 3119445 (D.S.C. Oct. 22, 2007).
Trademark law does not permit a trademark owner to use its trademark rights to silence commentary and criticism. As with news reporting, courts recognize the important First Amendment values at stake and usually deny efforts by trademark owners to encroach on legitimate commentary and criticism. There are several legal bases for this result: there is no risk of confusion between the commentator and the trademark owner, and nominative fair use may protect this use of the trademark owner's mark. Additionally, courts are likely to find that your use of a trademark in commentary or criticism is "not in connection with a good or service" and "noncommercial" (the argument is especially strong for the latter category). But note that some courts may find your use of a trademark for criticism and commentary to be commercial if you host advertising or link to commercial websites. In any event, to defeat a trademark dilution claim, you do not even need to show that your use is noncommercial. The federal dilution statute creates a categorical exemption for "criticizing . . . or commenting upon the famous mark owner or the goods or services of the famous mark owner." 15 U.S.C. § 1125(c)(3)(A)(ii). 🔥

snippet.title: Wegen Mordes: Lenkerin (18) bekommt lebenslängliche Haft | krone.tv NEWS
snippet.description: Im Juli vergangenen Jahres fuhr die damals 17-jährige Mackenzie, ohne abzubremsen, mit 160 km/h gegen ein Backsteingebäude. Bei dem Autounfall kamen ihr damaliger Freund Dominic Russo (20) sowie ein weiterer Freund, Davion Flanagan (19), ums Leben. Die Burschen waren auf der Stelle tot. Nur die Teenagerin überlebte. Dass es gar kein Unfall war, kam bald heraus: Eine Überwachungskamera zeichnete auf, wie die Lenkerin kurz dem Aufprall sogar noch auf das Gaspedal trat und beschleunigte.  Alle drei Insassen standen unter dem Einfluss von Marihuana. Auch Magic Mushrooms seien im Auto entdeckt worden.

snippet.title: You ain't no outlaw #outlaw #WildWest #movieclips #shorts #fyp
snippet.description:
You ain't no outlaw | The Ballad of Buster Scruggs

In this short clip from the Coen Brothers' western anthology "The Ballad of Buster Scruggs," we see Buster (played by Tim Blake Nelson) challenging a would-be outlaw (played by Clancy Brown) to a duel. But before the two can draw their guns, Buster makes a startling revelation about his opponent's true identity, much to the surprise of the other patrons at the saloon.
With its signature mix of irreverent humor and sudden violence, this scene encapsulates the quirky, unpredictable spirit of the film, which offers six distinct tales of life and death in the Old West. Whether you're a fan of classic westerns or just enjoy offbeat storytelling, "The Ballad of Buster Scruggs" is a must-see for anyone who loves movies that defy expectations.

snippet.title: Amoklauf an US-Schule: Der Weg der Killerin in Nashville | #shorts
snippet.description:
Wieder sorgt ein Amoklauf an einer Schule für Entsetzen, insgesamt 7 Menschen sterben, unter den 6 unschuldigen Menschen auch drei Kinder. Die Polizei identifiziert die Täterin als die 28-jährige Audrey Hale.

Sie wird 14 Minuten nach dem ersten Notruf von der Polizei erschossen. Nach Angaben der Polizei ist sie zuvor als Mann unterwegs, nennt sich Aiden. Ein Name, der auch auf einer der Tatwaffen steht. Sie soll selbst Schülerin an der christlichen Grundschule gewesen sein. Das Motiv ist weiter unklar. Die Polizei findet ein Manifest, in dem die Amokläuferin die Pläne für ihre Tat beschreibt.

Daarnaast vind je meerdere video’s per week met onder andere samenvattingen van Eredivisie en KKD wedstrijden, interviews met de beste voetbalspelers en de mooiste goals en grappigste fragmenten. snippet.title: Jesus Said | Secret Sayings of Jesus | Gospel of Thomas Saying : 13  #deadseascrolls
snippet.description:
Gospel of Thomas Saying  (13)
Jesus said to his disciples, "Compare me to someone and tell me whom I am like." Simon Peter said to him, "You are like a righteous angel." Matthew said to him, "You are like a wise philosopher." Thomas said to him, "Master, my mouth is wholly incapable of saying whom you are like." Jesus said, "I am not your master. Because you have drunk, you have become intoxicated from the bubbling spring which I have measured out." And he took him and withdrew and told him three things. When Thomas returned to his companions, they asked him, "What did Jesus say to you?" Thomas said to them, "If I tell you one of the things which he told me, you will pick up stones and throw them at me; a fire will come out of the stones and burn you up."
